aiashley — global digest Generated 06:48 UTC

Saturday, March 28, 2026

International News · 16 Sources · 4 Regions

Daily Brief

The US-Israeli war against Iran entered its second month with no clear diplomatic breakthrough, as Secretary of State Marco Rubio told G7 counterparts that Washington expects to conclude operations within weeks [France 24, RFI]. The conflict widened significantly with Yemen's Houthi rebels launching their first missile toward Israel since the war began [Le Monde, The Hindu], while an Iranian strike on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia wounded 12 US troops and damaged aircraft [Globe and Mail, Financial Times]. G7 foreign ministers issued a joint statement calling for an immediate end to attacks on civilians and urging Iran to restore free navigation through the Strait of Hormuz [RFI]. On the domestic front, the US faces mounting chaos as House Republicans rejected a Senate deal to fund the Department of Homeland Security, leaving TSA agents unpaid for over a month and causing widespread airport delays; President Trump signed an executive order to pay TSA workers as a stopgap [BBC, Globe and Mail]. In a separate development, the UN General Assembly passed a landmark resolution declaring the transatlantic slave trade 'the gravest crime against humanity,' with Western nations including the UK and EU abstaining [The Guardian, BBC]. Nepal's former Prime Minister KP Sharma Oli was arrested over his role in a deadly protest crackdown that killed over 70 people last year [BBC].

Top Stories
01 Conflict

US-Israeli War on Iran Enters Second Month as Houthis Fire First Missile at Israel

  • The Israeli military reported the first missile launch from Yemen toward Israeli territory since the war began on February 28, with aerial defense systems operating to intercept the threat
  • Yemen's Houthi movement warned it would join the conflict if US-Israeli attacks continue against Iran or if other nations enter the war
  • Secretary of State Marco Rubio stated the US expects to conclude military operations within weeks, not months, without deploying ground troops
  • The war has killed more than 1,937 people in Iran and displaced approximately one quarter of Lebanon's population
Coverage
Le MondeFocused on military developments and the Houthi warning France 24Emphasized US confidence in quick resolution and Lebanese PM interview Al JazeeraHighlighted civilian suffering in Lebanon and Iran, more critical of US-Israeli campaign The HinduNeutral reporting on military developments and regional implications RFIFocused on G7 diplomatic response and call for civilian protection
02 Conflict

Iranian Strike Wounds 12 US Troops at Saudi Air Base, Damages Aircraft

  • An Iranian attack on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia wounded 12 US service members, two of them seriously
  • Several aerial refueling planes were damaged in the strike, which involved at least one missile and several drones
  • The casualties bring the total US wounded since February 28 to over 300, with 13 US troops killed in the conflict
  • Iran agreed to allow humanitarian aid and agricultural shipments through the Strait of Hormuz following a UN request
Coverage
Globe and MailLed with the attack but also highlighted the humanitarian corridor breakthrough Financial TimesBrief coverage linking to Houthi developments The HinduStraightforward casualty reporting with regional context
03 Policy

US Airport Chaos Deepens as House Rejects DHS Funding Deal, Trump Signs Emergency Pay Order

  • House Republicans rejected a Senate deal to fund the Transportation Security Administration, extending the DHS partial shutdown to 44 days
  • TSA agents have not been paid for over a month, causing widespread delays at major US airports
  • President Trump signed an executive order directing payment to TSA employees, citing an emergency compromising national security
  • The order may face legal challenges as the Constitution assigns spending authority to Congress
Coverage
BBC WorldEmphasized political impasse and constitutional concerns Globe and MailFocused on Trump's executive action and practical implications for travelers
04 Geopolitics

UN General Assembly Declares Transatlantic Slave Trade 'Gravest Crime Against Humanity'

  • The UN General Assembly passed a resolution by 123 votes to 3, with 52 abstentions, declaring the transatlantic slave trade the gravest crime against humanity
  • The US, Argentina, and Israel voted against; the UK and EU member states abstained
  • Ghana's President John Mahama led the push for the resolution, which is not legally binding but strengthens momentum for reparations discussions
  • UN Secretary-General Guterres said Western nations' wealth was 'built on stolen lives and stolen labour'
Coverage
The GuardianExtensive analysis of voting patterns and reparations implications, highlighted African leadership BBC WorldBalanced explanation of the resolution's meaning and personal impact on descendants
05 Society

Nepal's Former PM Oli Arrested Over Deadly Protest Crackdown

  • Former Prime Minister KP Sharma Oli and ex-Home Minister Ramesh Lekhak were arrested following recommendations from an investigative panel
  • More than 70 people were killed during protests in September 2025, many shot by police during an uprising sparked by a social media ban
  • The arrests came one day after 35-year-old rapper-turned-politician Balen Shah was sworn in as new Prime Minister
  • Oli rejected the commission's findings as 'character assassination' and his lawyers said the detention was unwarranted
Coverage
BBC WorldComprehensive coverage including Oli's defense and context of political transition
Also Today
  • Tiger Woods arrested on DUI charges after rollover crash in Florida; authorities suspect drug impairment BBC World, Globe and Mail
  • EU reaches agreement to regulate flood of small parcels from Chinese e-commerce platforms Shein, Temu, and AliExpress Le Monde
  • Pope Leo XIV makes first papal visit to Monaco in nearly 500 years Le Monde, France 24
  • Bank of America agrees to pay $72.5 million to settle class-action lawsuit alleging facilitation of Epstein sex-trafficking ring Le Monde
  • Trump threatens Cuba with military action, saying 'Cuba is next' at Miami investment conference Al Jazeera, The Hindu
  • Two drone strikes in Sudan kill 28 civilians, including an infant, as civil war approaches fourth year The Guardian
  • Egypt imposes 9pm business curfew to counter energy costs that have more than doubled due to Iran war France 24, Financial Times
  • Pakistan and IMF reach staff-level agreement for disbursement of approximately $1.2 billion in funding The Hindu
  • Austria announces plans to ban social media for children under 14, joining growing international movement BBC Europe
  • Zelenskyy says US has linked security guarantees in peace deal to Ukraine ceding all of Donbas to Russia The Guardian
